claim: Three pillars of agent swarm infrastructure: runtime (largely solved via containers/VMs), orchestration/triggering (solved), and coordination (the missing gap where agents pick tasks and verify progress)
actionable: When building agent swarms, focus on solving the coordination problem: how agents signal task completion, verify SDLC stages, and proceed without human intervention via CI/coordination layer.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona

claim: VM-level isolation (not container isolation) required for secure multi-agent environments due to noisy neighbor problems in containers; Kubernetes pods cause compute contention
actionable: Deploy agent swarms using VM or micro-VM isolation (not containers) for security and resource guarantee; containers insufficient for production agent fleets.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona

claim: Coordination layer should be implemented as CLI gateway that local agents (Claude Code, etc.) can invoke to check progress and proceed through SDLC stages
actionable: Design coordination as CLI tool agents can call: 'cli check-stage dev', 'cli mark-complete', 'cli proceed'; enables deterministic workflow gating across multiple agents.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona

claim: GitHub is unsuitable as coordination layer for agent swarms (noisy with hundreds of parallel PRs); state machines and durable execution patterns better alternatives
actionable: Don't use GitHub as agent coordination layer; implement state machine workflow or durable execution framework to manage agent handoffs and stage transitions.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona

claim: Owner platform supports fleet pattern: agents spin up multiple VMs across repositories using triggers/schedules for tasks like CVE remediation or test coverage enforcement at scale
actionable: Use fleet infrastructure (Owner or equivalent) to trigger agents across repos on schedules; add prompts/scripts to agents.md to encode process knowledge for at-scale automation.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona

claim: Harness engineering: encoding SDLC process into repository context files (agents.md, skills, unit tests) helps agents understand workflow expectations and recover from getting lost
actionable: Write detailed agents.md documenting SDLC expectations; break down coarse SDLC stages into micro-steps agents can follow; update context when agents fail to follow workflow.
source_title: The Missing Primitive for Agent Swarms — Lou Bichard, Ona
